description_long: This object consists of two metal bottle caps, one green and one blue, each with visible white text printed on the top surface. The caps have a classic crimped edge design, typical of those used to seal glass beverage bottles. The green cap features a dense pattern of white text, while the blue cap displays a similar style of text, both likely indicating branding or product information. The metallic surfaces are slightly reflective, and the crimped edges show regular, evenly spaced ridges. There are no visible signs of damage, dents, or deformation on either cap. The undersides are not visible, but the overall appearance suggests they are standard bottle caps used for carbonated drinks or beer bottles.
description: Two metallic bottle caps, one green and one blue, each with white printed text and crimped edges, typical of beverage bottles.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the green cap in front and the blue cap behind, both with clear white text and crimped edges.
description_view_1: Side view with both caps lying flat, green on the right and blue on the left, highlighting the height and crimped edge details.
description_view_2: Angled view from the rear, showing the tops of both caps with their text and the metallic sheen.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, blue cap on the right and green on the left, emphasizing the crimped edges and flat tops.
attribute_label_or_branding: Both caps have white printed text on the top surface, with the green cap featuring dense white text and the blue cap displaying similar white text. The text appears to be branding or product information, but specific words are not fully legible.
